data.title: PHNIX Unveils airMono: The Revolutionary A+++ R290 Indoor Monoblock Heat Pump for Urban Retrofit
data.description: In Guangzhou, China, on April 17, 2026, PHNIX, a prominent heat pump producer, introduced airMono, a cutting-edge R290 Indoor Monoblock Heat Pump designed for European cities. This new product provides a 3-in-1 function of heating, cooling, and...

data.title: Akuvox X910 Claims Red Dot Award 2026: the World's First AI-powered Parcel Detection Smart Intercom Revolutionizes Luxury Home Access
data.description: In Xiamen, China on April 17, 2026, Akuvox, a top provider of smart home products, revealed that its X910 smart door phone received the prestigious Red Dot Award 2026 for Product Design. This recognition signifies the excellence of the flagship product in the global market. Akuvox's X910 stands out for its innovative design and functionality, making it a standout choice for consumers. The Red Dot Award is known worldwide as a symbol of outstanding design quality, highlighting the X910's exceptional features.

data.title: Netflix, State Street And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Friday
data.description: U.S. stock futures are trading with mixed results this morning, with investors likely focusing on certain stocks. State Street Corp (NYSE: STT) is anticipated to report quarterly earnings of $2.63 per share on revenue of $3.66 billion before the market opens. In after-hours trading, State Street shares dipped by 0.6% to $141.00. Meanwhile, Netflix Inc (NASDAQ: NFLX) exceeded expectations with its first-quarter financial results for 2026, which were released after the market closed on Thursday. Despite this, the company provided a disappointing forecast for the second quarter and disclosed that chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings will not seek re-election. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AutoFlight Completes First 2-Ton-Class eVTOL Tea Delivery in China
data.description: In Kunshan, China, on April 17th, 2026, AUTOFLIGHT achieved a milestone by conducting China's inaugural trial for the transportation of spring tea dedicated to eVTOLs in the 2-ton class. This groundbreaking trial took place in Guizhou, a rugged province in western China. The operation utilized the innovative CarryAll (V2000CG) by AutoFlight, an advanced, autonomous, and unmanned aircraft specifically designed for this task.

data.title: One Cell Beats Six! Amflow's Flagship, Powered by Ampace, Breaks the 700Wh Bottleneck
data.description: Shenzhen, China - April 17, 2026 - Amflow, a renowned e-bike manufacturer, has recently unveiled its latest premium PX series. The new flagship models, Amflow PX Carbon and PX Carbon Pro, feature the cutting-edge Ampace 50480 cylindrical lithium battery, offering enhanced performance. This launch marks a significant step for the brand's high-end product range, catering to bike enthusiasts seeking top-tier quality and innovation in their electric rides.

data.title: Ongwe Minerals Announces Listing on the Namibia Securities Exchange
data.description: Ongwe Minerals Inc. has successfully fulfilled all conditions for a dual-listing on the Namibia Securities Exchange, trading under the symbol “ONG” starting April 15, 2026. Ongwe’s primary listing on the TSX Venture Exchange (TSXV: OGW) will be retained. The company is excited to expand its presence, with the NSX (NSX: ONG) becoming another platform for investors. This move signifies Ongwe's strategic growth plans and commitment to providing opportunities for shareholders in multiple markets.

data.title: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On Wednesday? Potential Iran Deal Talks And Bank Earnings On Deck
data.description: On Tuesday, the S&P 500 saw a 1.18% rise, closing at 6,967.38, continuing its recent upward trend. This was influenced by decreasing oil prices and renewed optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran agreement. The Polymarket crowd, based on Polygon (CRYPTO: POL), is divided leading into Wednesday, with 51% of traders predicting the index will trend upward. This comes as the S&P 500 sits less than 1% below its record high of 7,002.28 from January, showcasing the market's significant recovery from Iran war-induced volatility. There is growing confidence among investors due to hints of progress in diplomatic efforts. President Trump stated that Tehran is interested in striking a deal, and a White House source mentioned discussions for a second meeting, though no formal arrangements have been made yet.

data.title: Janet Yellen Keeps Fed Cut Hopes Alive As Iran War Roils Markets— 'If I Had To Write One Thing Down...'
data.description: Janet Yellen, former U.S. Treasury Secretary, expressed her belief in the potential for one Federal Reserve rate cut later this year amidst the complications posed by an oil-driven inflation shock spurred by the Iran war. Speaking at the HSBC Global Investment Summit in Hong Kong, Yellen mentioned that despite the increase in near-term inflation expectations, the Fed is keeping an open mind. She stated, "If I had to write down one thing on a piece of paper, I suppose my guess would be that maybe there would be a cut later in the year," as per Reuters. Yellen pointed out that the over six-week conflict involving Iran is causing a widespread supply shock across the global economy, impacting commodities such as gasoline, LNG, fertilizers, food, and shipping costs. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Regions Financial Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Regions Financial Corporation (NYSE: RF) is set to unveil its first-quarter earnings on April 17. Analysts anticipate the Birmingham-based company to announce earnings of 59 cents per share, a rise from the previous year's 54 cents per share. The consensus estimate for the quarter's revenue stands at $1.92 billion, an increase from the previous year's $1.8 billion as per Benzinga Pro. Also, on April 15, the company declared dividends for both common and preferred stocks. The company's shares closed at $27.92 on Thursday, up by 0.3%. For further insights, readers can explore the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga to view the most-accurate analysts' ratings for the company.

data.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Ally Financial Expectations Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description: Ally Financial Inc. is set to announce first-quarter earnings on April 17 before the market opens. Analysts are predicting earnings per share of 94 cents, an increase from 58 cents in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to be around $2.14 billion, up from $1.54 billion in the previous year. This financial information comes from Benzinga Pro. Earlier this week, the company's board of directors approved a quarterly dividend of 30 cents per share for common stockholders. The stock price of Ally Financial closed at $41.96 on Thursday, showing a 0.7% decrease. For more details and analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on the Benzinga website.

data.title: Truist Financial Gears Up For Q1 Print; Here Are The Recent Forecast Changes From Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts
data.description: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE: TFC) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings report on Friday, April 17. Analysts are anticipating the Charlotte-based company to reveal earnings of $1.00 per share, an increase from 89 cents per share in the same quarter last year. The projected revenue for this quarter stands at $5.18 billion, compared to $4.95 billion reported last year. Truist Securities appointed Matthew Miller as the head of mergers & acquisitions on Feb. 26. The company's shares closed at $49.43 on Thursday, seeing a 0.3% drop. For further insights, readers can check out Benzinga's latest analyst ratings on their Analyst Stock Ratings page. Visit Benzinga.com for the complete story.
